title: facility — model relationships
description: Four owned entities. Facility is a leaf below Branch (which is under Region + Organization). ResourceBooking, DayPass, and Pass all attach to Facility (bookings) or Branch (day_passes) or Membership+Facility+Branch (passes). Every outbound FK stays same-tenant per tenancy-columns.md §5. NO organization_id / region_id / application_id on any owned row (facilities cascade all three through branch_id).

internal.description: Booking → facility relationship. RESTRICT on facility hard-delete via observer — booking rows must be cancelled before their facility can be purged.
RESTRICT on delete. Bookings anchor to their facility for auditing; a retired facility keeps its bookings for financial retention.
Facility-specific passes. Pass.facility_id NULL means the pass is valid at ANY facility in the branch.
Nullable — a pass may be branch-wide (facility_id=null) or facility-specific. RESTRICT on facility hard-delete.

outbound.description: Every facility is tenant-scoped per tenancy-columns.md §3.
RESTRICT: branches cannot be hard-deleted while facilities reference them. Branch archive is blocked by PreventFacilityOrphansOnBranchArchived hook when active facilities exist.
Denormalised tenant_id — cascades through facility.tenant_id but explicit for query-plan + FK integrity.
RESTRICT for audit-integrity — a user's hard-delete cannot orphan booking audit history.
For whom the booking is (parent booking for a child, receptionist for a member). Nullable for team bookings (booked_for_team_id) or self-bookings (equal to booked_by_user_id).
outbound.note: The observer denormalises booked_for_user_id = booked_by_user_id when nullable AND booked_for_team_id also null. This preserves a consistent 'for whom' semantic.

outbound.description: Wave 3 — team-level bookings (a coach reserving the pitch for team practice). Nullable in Wave 2 (Teams module lands in Wave 3).
The actor who cancelled (null for system cascades).
Day passes are branch-scoped, not facility-scoped — a walk-in visitor gets access to the whole branch.
Null = anonymous walk-in. The issued_to_profile_name + phone captured at issuance carry the identity in that case (redacted at 90 days per GDPR).
The staff member who sold the pass. RESTRICT for audit-integrity — sales staff hard-delete cannot orphan financial records.
Every pass is branch-scoped. Cross-branch passes are non-goals for Wave 2 (per module.json non-goals).
Wave 4 — the parent's paid Membership contract this pass belongs to. Nullable in Wave 2 (Finance module lands in Wave 4; passes issued manually in the meantime carry NULL).

inbound.description: Two downstream modules will attach to Facility in later waves:
examples: sports::Session (Wave 3) — belongs to Facility via BelongsToFacility trait. Practice sessions reserve a facility for a window.
sports::EventBooking (Wave 3) — belongs to Facility. Tournaments reserve N contiguous slots.
finance::Invoice (Wave 4) — line items reference ResourceBooking + DayPass + Pass for revenue attribution.
inbound.note: Facility does NOT have inbound relations in Wave 2 beyond the internal ones above.

notes: Facilities cascade Organization + Region through Branch. `facility.organization_id` and `facility.region_id` DO NOT EXIST — they are forbidden columns per tenancy-columns.md §5. Reach them via `$facility->branch->organization` / `$facility->branch->region`.
ResourceBooking does not carry branch_id — it cascades through facility_id → facility.branch_id. Same reasoning applies to region + organization.
DayPass carries branch_id directly because it's branch-scoped (not tied to any specific facility).
Pass carries branch_id + optional facility_id — the facility_id is nullable for branch-wide passes.
The `resource_bookings` table is the WRITE-side of Wave 3+ session scheduling. When Sports::Session lands in Wave 3, it composes ResourceBooking rather than duplicating the reservation primitive.
